claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 11 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 11 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my 2 children. I have Alex, who is 11 years old, and Blake, who is 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have two children: Alex (11) and Blake (15).

Now, let me ask about your living situation:

**Do you live in the UK, and do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK at the moment. And no, neither Alex nor Blake lives with me currently.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Since you don't currently live in the UK, I have a couple of follow-up questions:

**Are you living abroad in any of the following circumstances?**
- You are a Crown servant (for example, a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted overseas)
- You live in an EEA country or Switzerland and have certain connections to the UK

Please let me know which, if any, of these applies to you.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of those situations applies to me. I'm just living abroad - not as a Crown servant or anything like that, and I don't have those special connections you mentioned.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Based on what you've told me, I'm afraid the situation doesn't look favourable for Child Benefit eligibility, but let me just check one more thing to be thorough:

**Even though Alex and Blake don't live with you, are you currently contributing towards their upkeep (for example, paying for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation) at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest child and £16.95 per week for additional children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, I do send some money to help with their needs - you know, helping out with clothes and things like that. But to be honest, it's not very much. I send about £10 a week for Alex, and I'm not really able to contribute anything for Blake at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being so honest about your contributions.

To summarise your situation:

- You **do not live in the UK** and are not a Crown servant or living in a country with special arrangements
- **Neither Alex nor Blake lives with you**
- You contribute **£10 per week for Alex**, which is below the Child Benefit rate (£25.60 for the eldest or £16.95 for additional children)
- You are **not contributing anything for Blake**

Based on the eligibility rules, I now have enough information to make an assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm sorry to say that **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit** for either of your children.

---

### **Alex (age 11) – Not Eligible**
- You do not live in the UK and don't fall under any of the exceptions for claiming while abroad
- Alex does not live with you
- Your contribution of £10 per week is below the minimum required (at least £16.95–£25.60 per week) to qualify when a child lives with someone else

---

### **Blake (age 15) – Not Eligible**
- You do not live in the UK and don't fall under any of the exceptions for claiming while abroad
- Blake does not live with you
- You are not currently contributing towards Blake's upkeep

---

### **What are the main requirements?**
To normally qualify for Child Benefit, you need to:
1. **Live in the UK**, and
2. Either **live with the child** or **contribute at least the Child Benefit amount** towards their care

---

If your circumstances change in the future—for example, if you return to the UK or if the children come to live with you—you may wish to reassess your eligibility at that time.

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news, but please feel free to ask if you have any other questions.

reasonings: For Alex (age 11): The claimant does not live in the UK and does not qualify under any of the exceptions for living abroad (not a Crown servant, no special connection to EEA/Switzerland). Additionally, Alex does not live with the claimant. While the claimant does contribute towards Alex's upkeep (£10 per week), this amount is below the Child Benefit rate threshold (minimum £16.95 per week for an additional child, or £25.60 for the eldest). To qualify for Child Benefit when a child lives with someone else, the claimant must contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount. Since the claimant neither lives in the UK, nor lives with Alex, nor contributes the required minimum amount, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

For Blake (age 15): The claimant does not live in the UK and does not qualify under any of the exceptions for living abroad (not a Crown servant, no special connection to EEA/Switzerland). Additionally, Blake does not live with the claimant. The claimant is not contributing anything towards Blake's upkeep. To qualify for Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK and either live with the child or contribute at least the Child Benefit amount towards their care. Since the claimant neither lives in the UK, nor lives with Blake, nor contributes anything towards Blake's upkeep, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. The primary barrier is that the claimant does not live in the UK and does not fall under any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim while living abroad (such as being a Crown servant). Furthermore, neither child lives with the claimant, and the contributions made towards their upkeep do not meet the minimum threshold required to qualify when a child lives with someone else. For Alex, only £10 per week is contributed (below the minimum £16.95-£25.60 required), and for Blake, no contributions are being made at all.
